NKJV.1: Paul, called to be an apostle of Jesus Christ through the will of God, and Sosthenes our brother,
NKJV.2: To the church of God which is at Corinth, to those who are sanctified in Christ Jesus, called to be saints, with all who in every place call on the name of Jesus Christ our Lord, both theirs and ours:
NKJV.3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NKJV.4: I thank my God always concerning you for the grace of God which was given to you by Christ Jesus,
NKJV.5: that you were enriched in everything by Him in all utterance and all knowledge,
NKJV.6: even as the testimony of Christ was confirmed in you,
NKJV.7: so that you come short in no gift, eagerly waiting for the revelation of our Lord Jesus Christ,
NKJV.8: who will also confirm you to the end, that you may be blameless in the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NKJV.9: God is faithful, by whom you were called into the fellowship of His Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
NKJV.10: Now I plead with you, brethren, by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you all speak the same thing, and that there be no divisions among you, but that you be perfectly joined together in the same mind and in the same judgment.
NKJV.11: For it has been declared to me concerning you, my brethren, by those of Chloe’s household, that there are contentions among you.
NKJV.12: Now I say this, that each of you says, “I am of Paul,” or “I am of Apollos,” or “I am of Cephas,” or “I am of Christ.”
NKJV.13: Is Christ divided? Was Paul crucified for you? Or were you baptized in the name of Paul?
NKJV.14: I thank God that I baptized none of you except Crispus and Gaius,
NKJV.15: lest anyone should say that I had baptized in my own name.
NKJV.16: Yes, I also baptized the household of Stephanas. Besides, I do not know whether I baptized any other.
NKJV.17: For Christ did not send me to baptize, but to preach the gospel, not with wisdom of words, lest the cross of Christ should be made of no effect.
NKJV.18: For the message of the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.
NKJV.19: For it is written:  “I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, And bring to nothing the understanding of the prudent.”
NKJV.20: Where is the wise? Where is the scribe? Where is the disputer of this age? Has not God made foolish the wisdom of this world?
NKJV.21: For since, in the wisdom of God, the world through wisdom did not know God, it pleased God through the foolishness of the message preached to save those who believe.
NKJV.22: For Jews request a sign, and Greeks seek after wisdom;
NKJV.23: but we preach Christ crucified, to the Jews a stumbling block and to the Greeks foolishness,
NKJV.24: but to those who are called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ the power of God and the wisdom of God.
NKJV.25: Because the foolishness of God is wiser than men, and the weakness of God is stronger than men.
NKJV.26: For you see your calling, brethren, that not many wise according to the flesh, not many mighty, not many noble, are called.
NKJV.27: But God has chosen the foolish things of the world to put to shame the wise, and God has chosen the weak things of the world to put to shame the things which are mighty;
NKJV.28: and the base things of the world and the things which are despised God has chosen, and the things which are not, to bring to nothing the things that are,
NKJV.29: that no flesh should glory in His presence.
NKJV.30: But of Him you are in Christ Jesus, who became for us wisdom from God—and righteousness and sanctification and redemption—
NKJV.31: that, as it is written, “He who glories, let him glory in the LORD.”

ESV.1: Paul, called by the will of God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and our brother Sosthenes,
ESV.2: To the church of God that is in Corinth, to those sanctified in Christ Jesus, called to be saints together with all those who in every place call upon the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, both their Lord and ours:
ESV.3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
ESV.4: I give thanks to my God always for you because of the grace of God that was given you in Christ Jesus,
ESV.5: that in every way you were enriched in him in all speech and all knowledge —
ESV.6: even as the testimony about Christ was confirmed among you —
ESV.7: so that you are not lacking in any gift, as you wait for the revealing of our Lord Jesus Christ,
ESV.8: who will sustain you to the end, guiltless in the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
ESV.9: God is faithful, by whom you were called into the fellowship of his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
ESV.10: I appeal to you, brothers,  by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that all of you agree, and that there be no divisions among you, but that you be united in the same mind and the same judgment.
ESV.11: For it has been reported to me by Chloe's people that there is quarreling among you, my brothers.
ESV.12: What I mean is that each one of you says, “I follow Paul,” or “I follow Apollos,” or “I follow Cephas,” or “I follow Christ.”
ESV.13: Is Christ divided? Was Paul crucified for you? Or were you baptized in the name of Paul?
ESV.14: I thank God that I baptized none of you except Crispus and Gaius,
ESV.15: so that no one may say that you were baptized in my name.
ESV.16: (I did baptize also the household of Stephanas. Beyond that, I do not know whether I baptized anyone else.)
ESV.17: For Christ did not send me to baptize but to preach the gospel, and not with words of eloquent wisdom, lest the cross of Christ be emptied of its power.
ESV.18: For the word of the cross is folly to those who are perishing, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.
ESV.19: For it is written, “I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and the discernment of the discerning I will thwart.”
ESV.20: Where is the one who is wise? Where is the scribe? Where is the debater of this age? Has not God made foolish the wisdom of the world?
ESV.21: For since, in the wisdom of God, the world did not know God through wisdom, it pleased God through the folly of what we preach  to save those who believe.
ESV.22: For Jews demand signs and Greeks seek wisdom,
ESV.23: but we preach Christ crucified, a stumbling block to Jews and folly to Gentiles,
ESV.24: but to those who are called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ the power of God and the wisdom of God.
ESV.25: For the foolishness of God is wiser than men, and the weakness of God is stronger than men.
ESV.26: For consider your calling, brothers: not many of you were wise according to worldly standards,  not many were powerful, not many were of noble birth.
ESV.27: But God chose what is foolish in the world to shame the wise; God chose what is weak in the world to shame the strong;
ESV.28: God chose what is low and despised in the world, even things that are not, to bring to nothing things that are,
ESV.29: so that no human being  might boast in the presence of God.
ESV.30: And because of him  you are in Christ Jesus, who became to us wisdom from God, righteousness and sanctification and redemption,
ESV.31: so that, as it is written, “Let the one who boasts, boast in the Lord.”

NASB.1: Paul, called [as] an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Sosthenes our brother,
NASB.2: To the church of God which is at Corinth, to those who have been sanctified in Christ Jesus, saints by calling, with all who in every place call on the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, their [Lord] and ours:
NASB.3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NASB.4: I thank my God always concerning you for the grace of God which was given you in Christ Jesus,
NASB.5: that in everything you were enriched in Him, in all speech and all knowledge,
NASB.6: even as the testimony concerning Christ was confirmed in you,
NASB.7: so that you are not lacking in any gift, awaiting eagerly the revelation of our Lord Jesus Christ,
NASB.8: who will also confirm you to the end, blameless in the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NASB.9: God is faithful, through whom you were called into fellowship with His Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
NASB.10: Now I exhort you, brethren, by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you all agree and that there be no divisions among you, but that you be made complete in the same mind and in the same judgment.
NASB.11: For I have been informed concerning you, my brethren, by Chloe's [people], that there are quarrels among you.
NASB.12: Now I mean this, that each one of you is saying, 'I am of Paul,' and 'I of Apollos,' and 'I of Cephas,' and 'I of Christ.'
NASB.13: Has Christ been divided? Paul was not crucified for you, was he? Or were you baptized in the name of Paul?
NASB.14: I thank God that I baptized none of you except Crispus and Gaius,
NASB.15: so that no one would say you were baptized in my name.
NASB.16: Now I did baptize also the household of Stephanas; beyond that, I do not know whether I baptized any other.
NASB.17: For Christ did not send me to baptize, but to preach the gospel, not in cleverness of speech, so that the cross of Christ would not be made void.
NASB.18: For the word of the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.
NASB.19: For it is written, 'I WILL DESTROY THE WISDOM OF THE WISE, AND THE CLEVERNESS OF THE CLEVER I WILL SET ASIDE.'
NASB.20: Where is the wise man? Where is the scribe? Where is the debater of this age? Has not God made foolish the wisdom of the world?
NASB.21: For since in the wisdom of God the world through its wisdom did not [come to] know God, God was well-pleased through the foolishness of the message preached to save those who believe.
NASB.22: For indeed Jews ask for signs and Greeks search for wisdom;
NASB.23: but we preach Christ crucified, to Jews a stumbling block and to Gentiles foolishness,
NASB.24: but to those who are the called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ the power of God and the wisdom of God.
NASB.25: Because the foolishness of God is wiser than men, and the weakness of God is stronger than men.
NASB.26: For consider your calling, brethren, that there were not many wise according to the flesh, not many mighty, not many noble;
NASB.27: but God has chosen the foolish things of the world to shame the wise, and God has chosen the weak things of the world to shame the things which are strong,
NASB.28: and the base things of the world and the despised God has chosen, the things that are not, so that He may nullify the things that are,
NASB.29: so that no man may boast before God.
NASB.30: But by His doing you are in Christ Jesus, who became to us wisdom from God, and righteousness and sanctification, and redemption,
NASB.31: so that, just as it is written, 'LET HIM WHO BOASTS, BOAST IN THE LORD.'

NLT.1: This letter is from Paul, chosen by the will of God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from our brother Sosthenes.
NLT.2: I am writing to God’s church in Corinth, to you who have been called by God to be his own holy people. He made you holy by means of Christ Jesus, just as he did for all people everywhere who call on the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, their Lord and ours.
NLT.3: May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
NLT.4: I always thank my God for you and for the gracious gifts he has given you, now that you belong to Christ Jesus.
NLT.5: Through him, God has enriched your church in every way — with all of your eloquent words and all of your knowledge.
NLT.6: This confirms that what I told you about Christ is true.
NLT.7: Now you have every spiritual gift you need as you eagerly wait for the return of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NLT.8: He will keep you strong to the end so that you will be free from all blame on the day when our Lord Jesus Christ returns.
NLT.9: God will do this, for he is faithful to do what he says, and he has invited you into partnership with his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
NLT.10: I appeal to you, dear brothers and sisters, by the authority of our Lord Jesus Christ, to live in harmony with each other. Let there be no divisions in the church. Rather, be of one mind, united in thought and purpose.
NLT.11: For some members of Chloe’s household have told me about your quarrels, my dear brothers and sisters.
NLT.12: Some of you are saying, “I am a follower of Paul.” Others are saying, “I follow Apollos,” or “I follow Peter, ” or “I follow only Christ.”
NLT.13: Has Christ been divided into factions? Was I, Paul, crucified for you? Were any of you baptized in the name of Paul? Of course not!
NLT.14: I thank God that I did not baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius,
NLT.15: for now no one can say they were baptized in my name.
NLT.16: (Oh yes, I also baptized the household of Stephanas, but I don’t remember baptizing anyone else.)
NLT.17: For Christ didn’t send me to baptize, but to preach the Good News — and not with clever speech, for fear that the cross of Christ would lose its power.
NLT.18: The message of the cross is foolish to those who are headed for destruction! But we who are being saved know it is the very power of God.
NLT.19:
As the Scriptures say, “I will destroy the wisdom of the wise
and discard the intelligence of the intelligent.”
NLT.20: So where does this leave the philosophers, the scholars, and the world’s brilliant debaters? God has made the wisdom of this world look foolish.
NLT.21: Since God in his wisdom saw to it that the world would never know him through human wisdom, he has used our foolish preaching to save those who believe.
NLT.22: It is foolish to the Jews, who ask for signs from heaven. And it is foolish to the Greeks, who seek human wisdom.
NLT.23: So when we preach that Christ was crucified, the Jews are offended and the Gentiles say it’s all nonsense.
NLT.24: But to those called by God to salvation, both Jews and Gentiles, Christ is the power of God and the wisdom of God.
NLT.25: This foolish plan of God is wiser than the wisest of human plans, and God’s weakness is stronger than the greatest of human strength.
NLT.26: Remember, dear brothers and sisters, that few of you were wise in the world’s eyes or powerful or wealthy when God called you.
NLT.27: Instead, God chose things the world considers foolish in order to shame those who think they are wise. And he chose things that are powerless to shame those who are powerful.
NLT.28: God chose things despised by the world, things counted as nothing at all, and used them to bring to nothing what the world considers important.
NLT.29: As a result, no one can ever boast in the presence of God.
NLT.30: God has united you with Christ Jesus. For our benefit God made him to be wisdom itself. Christ made us right with God; he made us pure and holy, and he freed us from sin.
NLT.31: Therefore, as the Scriptures say, “If you want to boast, boast only about the LORD.”

WEB.1: Paul, called to be an apostle of Jesus Christ through the will of God, and our brother Sosthenes,
WEB.2: to the assembly of God which is at Corinth; those who are sanctified in Christ Jesus, called to be saints, with all who call on the name of our Lord Jesus Christ in every place, both theirs and ours:
WEB.3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
WEB.4: I always thank my God concerning you, for the grace of God which was given you in Christ Jesus;
WEB.5: that in everything you were enriched in him, in all speech and all knowledge;
WEB.6: even as the testimony of Christ was confirmed in you:
WEB.7: so that you come behind in no gift; waiting for the revelation of our Lord Jesus Christ;
WEB.8: who will also confirm you until the end, blameless in the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
WEB.9: God is faithful, through whom you were called into the fellowship of his Son, Jesus Christ, our Lord.
WEB.10: Now I beg you, brothers,  through the name of our Lord, Jesus Christ, that you all speak the same thing, and that there be no divisions among you, but that you be perfected together in the same mind and in the same judgment.
WEB.11: For it has been reported to me concerning you, my brothers, by those who are from Chloe’s household, that there are contentions among you.
WEB.12: Now I mean this, that each one of you says, “I follow Paul,” “I follow Apollos,” “I follow Cephas,” and, “I follow Christ.”
WEB.13: Is Christ divided? Was Paul crucified for you? Or were you baptized into the name of Paul?
WEB.14: I thank God that I baptized none of you, except Crispus and Gaius,
WEB.15: so that no one should say that I had baptized you into my own name.
WEB.16: (I also baptized the household of Stephanas; besides them, I don’t know whether I baptized any other.)
WEB.17: For Christ sent me not to baptize, but to preach the Good News — not in wisdom of words, so that the cross of Christ wouldn’t be made void.
WEB.18: For the word of the cross is foolishness to those who are dying, but to us who are saved it is the power of God.
WEB.19: For it is written, “I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, I will bring the discernment of the discerning to nothing.”
WEB.20: Where is the wise? Where is the scribe? Where is the lawyer of this world? Hasn’t God made foolish the wisdom of this world?
WEB.21: For seeing that in the wisdom of God, the world through its wisdom didn’t know God, it was God’s good pleasure through the foolishness of the preaching to save those who believe.
WEB.22: For Jews ask for signs, Greeks seek after wisdom,
WEB.23: but we preach Christ crucified; a stumbling block to Jews, and foolishness to Greeks,
WEB.24: but to those who are called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ is the power of God and the wisdom of God.
WEB.25: Because the foolishness of God is wiser than men, and the weakness of God is stronger than men.
WEB.26: For you see your calling, brothers, that not many are wise according to the flesh, not many mighty, and not many noble;
WEB.27: but God chose the foolish things of the world that he might put to shame those who are wise. God chose the weak things of the world, that he might put to shame the things that are strong;
WEB.28: and God chose the lowly things of the world, and the things that are despised, and the things that are not, that he might bring to nothing the things that are:
WEB.29: that no flesh should boast before God.
WEB.30: Because of him, you are in Christ Jesus, who was made to us wisdom from God, and righteousness and sanctification, and redemption:
WEB.31: that, according as it is written, “He who boasts, let him boast in the Lord.”

CSB17.1: Paul, <sup>ⓘ</sup> called as an apostle <sup>ⓙ</sup> of Christ Jesus by God’s will, <sup>ⓚ</sup> and Sosthenes our brother: <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.2: To the church of God at Corinth, <sup>ⓜ</sup> to those sanctified <sup>ⓝ</sup> in Christ Jesus, called <sup>ⓐ</sup> as saints, with all those in every place who call on the name <sup>ⓑ</sup> of Jesus Christ our Lord — both their Lord and ours.
CSB17.3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father <sup>ⓒ</sup> and the Lord Jesus Christ.
CSB17.4: I always thank <sup>ⓓ</sup> my God for you because of the grace of God given to you in Christ Jesus,
CSB17.5: that you were enriched in him <sup>ⓔ</sup> in every way, in all speech and all knowledge. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.6: In this way, the testimony about Christ was confirmed among you, <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.7: so that you do not lack any spiritual gift as you eagerly wait <sup>ⓗ</sup> for the revelation <sup>ⓘ</sup> of our Lord Jesus Christ.
CSB17.8: He will also strengthen you to the end, <sup>ⓙ</sup> so that you will be blameless in the day of our Lord Jesus Christ. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.9: God is faithful; <sup>ⓛ</sup> you were called by him <sup>ⓜ</sup> into fellowship with his Son, <sup>ⓝ</sup> Jesus Christ our Lord.
CSB17.10: Now I urge you, brothers and sisters, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that all of you agree in what you say, that there be no divisions among you, and that you be united with the same understanding and the same conviction.
CSB17.11: For it has been reported to me about you, my brothers and sisters, by members of Chloe’s people, that there is rivalry <sup>ⓐ</sup> among you.
CSB17.12: What I am saying is this: One of you says, “I belong to Paul,” or “I belong to Apollos,” <sup>ⓑ</sup> or “I belong to Cephas,” <sup>ⓒ</sup> or “I belong to Christ.” <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.13: Is Christ divided? <sup>ⓔ</sup> Was Paul crucified for you? Or were you baptized in Paul’s name?
CSB17.14: I thank God <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>[2]</sup> that I baptized none of you except Crispus <sup>ⓕ</sup> and Gaius, <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.15: so that no one can say you were baptized in my name.
CSB17.16: I did, in fact, baptize the household <sup>ⓗ</sup> of Stephanas; <sup>ⓘ</sup> beyond that, I don’t recall if I baptized anyone else.
CSB17.17: For Christ did not send me to baptize, but to preach the gospel — not with eloquent wisdom, so that the cross <sup>ⓙ</sup> of Christ will not be emptied of its effect.
CSB17.18: For the word of the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing, but it is the power of God to us who are being saved. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.19:
For it is written, I will destroy the wisdom of the wise,
and I will set aside the intelligence of the intelligent. ⓛ ⓜ
CSB17.20: Where is the one who is wise? Where is the teacher of the law? <sup>[3]</sup> Where is the debater of this age? <sup>ⓝ</sup> Hasn’t God made the world’s wisdom foolish?
CSB17.21: For since, in God’s wisdom, the world did not know God through wisdom, God was pleased to save those who believe through the foolishness of what is preached.
CSB17.22: For the Jews ask for signs <sup>ⓐ</sup> and the Greeks seek wisdom,
CSB17.23: but we preach Christ crucified, <sup>ⓑ</sup> a stumbling block to the Jews and foolishness to the Gentiles. <sup>[4]</sup>
CSB17.24: Yet to those who are called, <sup>ⓒ</sup> both Jews and Greeks, Christ is the power of God and the wisdom of God,
CSB17.25: because God’s foolishness is wiser than human wisdom, and God’s weakness is stronger than human strength.
CSB17.26: Brothers and sisters, consider your calling: Not many were wise from a human perspective, <sup>[5]</sup> not many powerful, <sup>ⓓ</sup> not many of noble birth.
CSB17.27: Instead, God has chosen <sup>ⓔ</sup> what is foolish in the world to shame the wise, and God has chosen what is weak in the world to shame the strong.
CSB17.28: God has chosen what is insignificant and despised in the world <sup>ⓕ</sup>  — what is viewed as nothing — to bring to nothing what is viewed as something,
CSB17.29: so that no one <sup>[6]</sup> may boast in his presence. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.30: It is from him that you are in Christ Jesus, who became wisdom from God for us — our righteousness, <sup>ⓗ</sup> sanctification, <sup>ⓘ</sup> and redemption, <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.31: in order that, as it is written: ⓚ Let the one who boasts, boast in the Lord. [7] ⓛ

AMP.1: Paul, called as an apostle (special messenger, personally chosen representative) of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and our brother Sosthenes,
AMP.2: To the church of God in Corinth, to those sanctified (set apart, made holy) in Christ Jesus, who are selected and called as saints (God's people), together with all those who in every place call on and honor the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, their Lord and ours:
AMP.3: Grace to you and peace [inner calm and spiritual well-being] from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
AMP.4: I thank my God always for you because of the grace of God which was given you in Christ Jesus,
AMP.5: so that in everything you were [exceedingly] enriched in Him, in all speech [empowered by the spiritual gifts] and in all knowledge [with insight into the faith].
AMP.6: In this way our testimony about Christ was confirmed and established in you,
AMP.7: so that you are not lacking in any spiritual gift [which comes from the Holy Spirit], as you eagerly wait [with confident trust] for the revelation of our Lord Jesus Christ [when He returns].
AMP.8: And He will also confirm you to the end [keeping you strong and free of any accusation, so that you will be] blameless and beyond reproach in the day [of the return] of our Lord Jesus Christ.
AMP.9: God is faithful [He is reliable, trustworthy and ever true to His promise—He can be depended on], and through Him you were called into fellowship with His Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
AMP.10: But I urge you, believers, by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that all of you be in full agreement in what you say, and that there be no divisions or factions among you, but that you be perfectly united in your way of thinking and in your judgment [about matters of the faith].
AMP.11: For I have been informed about you, my brothers and sisters, by those of Chloe's household, that there are quarrels and factions among you.
AMP.12: Now I mean this, that each one of you says, "I am [a disciple] of Paul," or "I am [a disciple] of Apollos," or "I am [a disciple] of Cephas (Peter)," or "I am [a disciple] of Christ."
AMP.13: Has Christ been divided [into different parts]? Was Paul crucified for you? Or were you baptized into the name of Paul? [Certainly not!]
AMP.14: I thank God that I did not baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius,
AMP.15: so that no one would say that you were baptized into my name.
AMP.16: Now I also baptized the household of Stephanas; beyond that, I do not know if I baptized anyone else.
AMP.17: For Christ did not send me [as an apostle] to baptize, but [commissioned and empowered me] to preach the good news [of salvation]—not with clever and eloquent speech [as an orator], so that the cross of Christ would not be made ineffective [deprived of its saving power].
AMP.18: For the message of the cross is foolishness [absurd and illogical] to those who are perishing and spiritually dead [because they reject it], but to us who are being saved [by God's grace] it is [the manifestation of] the power of God.
AMP.19:
For it is written and forever remains written,
"I will destroy the wisdom of the wise [the philosophy of the philosophers],
And the cleverness of the clever [who do not know Me] I will nullify."
AMP.20: Where is the wise man (philosopher)? Where is the scribe (scholar)? Where is the debater (logician, orator) of this age? Has God not exposed the foolishness of this world's wisdom?
AMP.21: For since the world through all its [earthly] wisdom failed to recognize God, God in His wisdom was well-pleased through the foolishness of the message preached [regarding salvation] to save those who believe [in Christ and welcome Him as Savior].
AMP.22: For Jews demand signs (attesting miracles), and Greeks pursue [worldly] wisdom and philosophy,
AMP.23: but we preach Christ crucified, [a message which is] to Jews a stumbling block [that provokes their opposition], and to Gentiles foolishness [just utter nonsense],
AMP.24: but to those who are the called, both Jews and Greeks (Gentiles), Christ is the power of God and the wisdom of God.
AMP.25: [This is] because the foolishness of God [is not foolishness at all and] is wiser than men [far beyond human comprehension], and the weakness of God is stronger than men [far beyond the limits of human effort].
AMP.26: Just look at your own calling, believers; not many [of you were considered] wise according to human standards, not many powerful or influential, not many of high and noble birth.
AMP.27: But God has selected [for His purpose] the foolish things of the world to shame the wise [revealing their ignorance], and God has selected [for His purpose] the weak things of the world to shame the things which are strong [revealing their frailty].
AMP.28: God has selected [for His purpose] the insignificant (base) things of the world, and the things that are despised and treated with contempt, [even] the things that are nothing, so that He might reduce to nothing the things that are,
AMP.29: so that no one may [be able to] boast in the presence of God.
AMP.30: But it is from Him that you are in Christ Jesus, who became to us wisdom from God [revealing His plan of salvation], and righteousness [making us acceptable to God], and sanctification [making us holy and setting us apart for God], and redemption [providing our ransom from the penalty for sin],
AMP.31: so then, as it is written [in Scripture], "He who boasts and glories, let him boast and glory in the Lord."

MSG.1: I, Paul, have been called and sent by Jesus, the Messiah, according to God's plan, along with my friend Sosthenes.
MSG.2: I send this letter to you in God's church at Corinth, Christians cleaned up by Jesus and set apart for a God-filled life. I include in my greeting all who call out to Jesus, wherever they live. He's their Master as well as ours!
MSG.3: May all the gifts and benefits that come from God our Father, and the Master, Jesus Christ, be yours.
MSG.4: Every time I think of you—and I think of you often!—I thank God for your lives of free and open access to God, given by Jesus.
MSG.5: There's no end to what has happened in you—it's beyond speech, beyond knowledge.
MSG.6: The evidence of Christ has been clearly verified in your lives.
MSG.7: Just think—you don't need a thing, you've got it all! All God's gifts are right in front of you as you wait expectantly for our Master Jesus to arrive on the scene for the Finale.
MSG.8: And not only that, but God himself is right alongside to keep you steady and on track until things are all wrapped up by Jesus.
MSG.9: God, who got you started in this spiritual adventure, shares with us the life of his Son and our Master Jesus. He will never give up on you. Never forget that.
MSG.10: I have a serious concern to bring up with you, my friends, using the authority of Jesus, our Master. I'll put it as urgently as I can: You must get along with each other. You must learn to be considerate of one another, cultivating a life in common.
MSG.11: I bring this up because some from Chloe's family brought a most disturbing report to my attention—that you're fighting among yourselves!
MSG.12: I'll tell you exactly what I was told: You're all picking sides, going around saying, "I'm on Paul's side," or "I'm for Apollos," or "Peter is my man," or "I'm in the Messiah group."
MSG.13: I ask you, "Has the Messiah been chopped up in little pieces so we can each have a relic all our own? Was Paul crucified for you? Was a single one of you baptized in Paul's name?"
MSG.14: I was not involved with any of your baptisms—except for Crispus and Gaius—and on getting this report, I'm sure glad I wasn't.
MSG.15: At least no one can go around saying he was baptized in my name.
MSG.16: (Come to think of it, I also baptized Stephanas's family, but as far as I can recall, that's it.)
MSG.17: God didn't send me out to collect a following for myself, but to preach the Message of what he has done, collecting a following for him. And he didn't send me to do it with a lot of fancy rhetoric of my own, lest the powerful action at the center—Christ on the Cross—be trivialized into mere words.
MSG.18: The Message that points to Christ on the Cross seems like sheer silliness to those hellbent on destruction, but for those on the way of salvation it makes perfect sense. This is the way God works, and most powerfully as it turns out.
MSG.19: It's written, I'll turn conventional wisdom on its head, I'll expose so-called experts as crackpots.
MSG.20: So where can you find someone truly wise, truly educated, truly intelligent in this day and age? Hasn't God exposed it all as pretentious nonsense?
MSG.21: Since the world in all its fancy wisdom never had a clue when it came to knowing God, God in his wisdom took delight in using what the world considered dumb—preaching, of all things!—to bring those who trust him into the way of salvation.
MSG.22: While Jews clamor for miraculous demonstrations and Greeks go in for philosophical wisdom,
MSG.23: we go right on proclaiming Christ, the Crucified. Jews treat this like an anti-miracle—and Greeks pass it off as absurd.
MSG.24: But to us who are personally called by God himself—both Jews and Greeks—Christ is God's ultimate miracle and wisdom all wrapped up in one.
MSG.25: Human wisdom is so tinny, so impotent, next to the seeming absurdity of God. Human strength can't begin to compete with God's "weakness."
MSG.26: Take a good look, friends, at who you were when you got called into this life. I don't see many of "the brightest and the best" among you, not many influential, not many from high-society families.
MSG.27: Isn't it obvious that God deliberately chose men and women that the culture overlooks and exploits and abuses,
MSG.28: chose these "nobodies" to expose the hollow pretensions of the "somebodies"?
MSG.29: That makes it quite clear that none of you can get by with blowing your own horn before God.
MSG.30: Everything that we have—right thinking and right living, a clean slate and a fresh start—comes from God by way of Jesus Christ.
MSG.31: That's why we have the saying, "If you're going to blow a horn, blow a trumpet for God."

NIV.1: Paul, called to be an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and our brother Sosthenes,
NIV.2: To the church of God in Corinth, to those sanctified in Christ Jesus and called to be holy, together with all those everywhere who call on the name of our Lord Jesus Christ—their Lord and ours:
NIV.3: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NIV.4:
Thanksgiving
I always thank God for you because of his grace given you in Christ Jesus.
NIV.5: For in him you have been enriched in every way—in all your speaking and in all your knowledge—
NIV.6: because our testimony about Christ was confirmed in you.
NIV.7: Therefore you do not lack any spiritual gift as you eagerly wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to be revealed.
NIV.8: He will keep you strong to the end, so that you will be blameless on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NIV.9: God, who has called you into fellowship with his Son Jesus Christ our Lord, is faithful.
NIV.10:
Divisions in the Church
I appeal to you, brothers, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that all of you agree with one another so that there may be no divisions among you and that you may be perfectly united in mind and thought.
NIV.11: My brothers, some from Chloe’s household have informed me that there are quarrels among you.
NIV.12: What I mean is this: One of you says, “I follow Paul”; another, “I follow Apollos”; another, “I follow Cephas”; still another, “I follow Christ.”
NIV.13: Is Christ divided? Was Paul crucified for you? Were you baptized into the name of Paul?
NIV.14: I am thankful that I did not baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius,
NIV.15: so no one can say that you were baptized into my name.
NIV.16: (Yes, I also baptized the household of Stephanas; beyond that, I don’t remember if I baptized anyone else.)
NIV.17: For Christ did not send me to baptize, but to preach the gospel—not with words of human wisdom, lest the cross of Christ be emptied of its power.
NIV.18:
Christ the Wisdom and Power of God
For the message of the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.
NIV.19:
For it is written:
“I will destroy the wisdom of the wise;
the intelligence of the intelligent I will frustrate.”
NIV.20: Where is the wise man? Where is the scholar? Where is the philosopher of this age? Has not God made foolish the wisdom of the world?
NIV.21: For since in the wisdom of God the world through its wisdom did not know him, God was pleased through the foolishness of what was preached to save those who believe.
NIV.22: Jews demand miraculous signs and Greeks look for wisdom,
NIV.23: but we preach Christ crucified: a stumbling block to Jews and foolishness to Gentiles,
NIV.24: but to those whom God has called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ the power of God and the wisdom of God.
NIV.25: For the foolishness of God is wiser than man’s wisdom, and the weakness of God is stronger than man’s strength.
NIV.26: Brothers, think of what you were when you were called. Not many of you were wise by human standards; not many were influential; not many were of noble birth.
NIV.27: But God chose the foolish things of the world to shame the wise; God chose the weak things of the world to shame the strong.
NIV.28: He chose the lowly things of this world and the despised things—and the things that are not—to nullify the things that are,
NIV.29: so that no one may boast before him.
NIV.30: It is because of him that you are in Christ Jesus, who has become for us wisdom from God—that is, our righteousness, holiness and redemption.
NIV.31: Therefore, as it is written: “Let him who boasts boast in the Lord.”

NRSVCE.1: Paul, called to be an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and our brother Sosthenes,
NRSVCE.2: To the church of God that is in Corinth, to those who are sanctified in Christ Jesus, called to be saints, together with all those who in every place call on the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, both their Lord and ours:
NRSVCE.3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.4: I give thanks to my God always for you because of the grace of God that has been given you in Christ Jesus,
NRSVCE.5: for in every way you have been enriched in him, in speech and knowledge of every kind —
NRSVCE.6: just as the testimony of Christ has been strengthened among you —
NRSVCE.7: so that you are not lacking in any spiritual gift as you wait for the revealing of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.8: He will also strengthen you to the end, so that you may be blameless on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.9: God is faithful; by him you were called into the fellowship of his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
NRSVCE.10: Now I appeal to you, brothers and sisters, by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that all of you be in agreement and that there be no divisions among you, but that you be united in the same mind and the same purpose.
NRSVCE.11: For it has been reported to me by Chloe’s people that there are quarrels among you, my brothers and sisters.
NRSVCE.12: What I mean is that each of you says, “I belong to Paul,” or “I belong to Apollos,” or “I belong to Cephas,” or “I belong to Christ.”
NRSVCE.13: Has Christ been divided? Was Paul crucified for you? Or were you baptized in the name of Paul?
NRSVCE.14: I thank God that I baptized none of you except Crispus and Gaius,
NRSVCE.15: so that no one can say that you were baptized in my name.
NRSVCE.16: (I did baptize also the household of Stephanas; beyond that, I do not know whether I baptized anyone else.)
NRSVCE.17: For Christ did not send me to baptize but to proclaim the gospel, and not with eloquent wisdom, so that the cross of Christ might not be emptied of its power.
NRSVCE.18: For the message about the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.
NRSVCE.19:
For it is written, “I will destroy the wisdom of the wise,
and the discernment of the discerning I will thwart.”
NRSVCE.20: Where is the one who is wise? Where is the scribe? Where is the debater of this age? Has not God made foolish the wisdom of the world?
NRSVCE.21: For since, in the wisdom of God, the world did not know God through wisdom, God decided, through the foolishness of our proclamation, to save those who believe.
NRSVCE.22: For Jews demand signs and Greeks desire wisdom,
NRSVCE.23: but we proclaim Christ crucified, a stumbling block to Jews and foolishness to Gentiles,
NRSVCE.24: but to those who are the called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ the power of God and the wisdom of God.
NRSVCE.25: For God’s foolishness is wiser than human wisdom, and God’s weakness is stronger than human strength.
NRSVCE.26: Consider your own call, brothers and sisters: not many of you were wise by human standards, not many were powerful, not many were of noble birth.
NRSVCE.27: But God chose what is foolish in the world to shame the wise; God chose what is weak in the world to shame the strong;
NRSVCE.28: God chose what is low and despised in the world, things that are not, to reduce to nothing things that are,
NRSVCE.29: so that no one might boast in the presence of God.
NRSVCE.30: He is the source of your life in Christ Jesus, who became for us wisdom from God, and righteousness and sanctification and redemption,
NRSVCE.31: in order that, as it is written, “Let the one who boasts, boast in the Lord.”

NET.1: From Paul, called to be an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Sosthenes, our brother,
NET.2: to the church of God that is in Corinth, to those who are sanctified in Christ Jesus, and called to be saints, with all those in every place who call on the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, their Lord and ours.
NET.3: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ!
NET.4: I always thank my God for you because of the grace of God that was given to you in Christ Jesus.
NET.5: For you were made rich in every way in him, in all your speech and in every kind of knowledge —
NET.6: just as the testimony about Christ has been confirmed among you —
NET.7: so that you do not lack any spiritual gift as you wait for the revelation of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NET.8: He will also strengthen you to the end, so that you will be blameless on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NET.9: God is faithful, by whom you were called into fellowship with his son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
NET.10: I urge you, brothers and sisters, by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, to agree together, to end your divisions, and to be united by the same mind and purpose.
NET.11: For members of Chloe’s household have made it clear to me, my brothers and sisters, that there are quarrels among you.
NET.12: Now I mean this, that each of you is saying, “I am with Paul,” or “I am with Apollos,” or “I am with Cephas,” or “I am with Christ.”
NET.13: Is Christ divided? Paul wasn’t crucified for you, was he? Or were you in fact baptized in the name of Paul?
NET.14: I thank God that I did not baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius,
NET.15: so that no one can say that you were baptized in my name!
NET.16: (I also baptized the household of Stephanus. Otherwise, I do not remember whether I baptized anyone else.)
NET.17: For Christ did not send me to baptize, but to preach the gospel — and not with clever speech, so that the cross of Christ would not become useless.
NET.18: For the message about the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.
NET.19: For it is written, “I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and I will thwart the cleverness of the intelligent.”
NET.20: Where is the wise man? Where is the expert in the Mosaic law? Where is the debater of this age? Has God not made the wisdom of the world foolish?
NET.21: For since in the wisdom of God the world by its wisdom did not know God, God was pleased to save those who believe by the foolishness of preaching.
NET.22: For Jews demand miraculous signs and Greeks ask for wisdom,
NET.23: but we preach about a crucified Christ, a stumbling block to Jews and foolishness to Gentiles.
NET.24: But to those who are called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ is the power of God and the wisdom of God.
NET.25: For the foolishness of God is wiser than human wisdom, and the weakness of God is stronger than human strength.
NET.26: Think about the circumstances of your call, brothers and sisters. Not many were wise by human standards, not many were powerful, not many were born to a privileged position.
NET.27: But God chose what the world thinks foolish to shame the wise, and God chose what the world thinks weak to shame the strong.
NET.28: God chose what is low and despised in the world, what is regarded as nothing, to set aside what is regarded as something,
NET.29: so that no one can boast in his presence.
NET.30: He is the reason you have a relationship with Christ Jesus, who became for us wisdom from God, and righteousness and sanctification and redemption,
NET.31: so that, as it is written, “Let the one who boasts, boast in the Lord.”
